zh.lifeSummary: 當Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen於1180年5月15日出生在比利時安特維爾梅倫時，她的父親Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard21歲，母親Sophia van Loon17歲。她於大約1216年與Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT在荷蘭布拉班特提爾包格結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。她去世於1243年4月10日在荷蘭努-布拉班特提爾包格享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen于1180年5月15日出生在比利时安特維爾梅倫时，她的父亲Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard21岁，母亲Sophia van Loon17岁。她于大约1216年与Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT在荷兰布拉班特提爾包格结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。她去世于1243年4月10日在荷兰努-布拉班特提爾包格享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Waltherus
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: patronymic from the personal name Walther (see Walter ).

variants.name: Walter
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

en.lifeSummary: When Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen was born on 15 May 1180, in Mechelen, Antwerp, Belgium, her father, Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard, was 21 and her mother, Sophia van Loon, was 17. She married Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT about 1216, in Tilburg, Brabant, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. She died on 10 April 1243, in Tilburg, North Brabant, Netherlands, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen est née le 15 mai 1180, à Mechelen, Anvers, Belgique, son père, Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Sophia van Loon, avait 17 ans. Elle a épousé Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT environ 1216, à Tilburg, Brabant, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Elle est décédée le 10 avril 1243, à Tilburg, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen am 15. Mai 1180 in Mechelen, Antwerpen, Belgien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard 21 und ihre Mutter Sophia van Loon 17. Sie hat um 1216 in Tilburg, Brabant, Niederlande Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb am 10. April 1243 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Tilburg, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen è nata il il 15 maggio 1180 nel Mechelen, Anversa, Belgio, sua padre, Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard, aveva 21 anni e sua madre, Sophia van Loon, aveva 17 anni. Lei sposato Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT circa 1216, nel Tilburg, Brabant, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì il 10 aprile 1243, nel Tilburg, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergenが1180年5月15日にベルギーアントウエルペンメチェレンで生まれた時，父Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerardは21歳，母Sophia van Loonは17歳であった。約1216年にオランダブラバントティルブルグでArnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOTと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼女は1243年4月10日にオランダノオルド-ブラバントティルブルグで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen(이)가 1180년 5월 15일 벨기에 앤트워펜 미셸에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard(은)는 21세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sophia van Loon(은)는 17세였다. 그녀는 대략 1216년 네덜란드 브라반트 틸버그에서 Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1243년 4월 10일 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 틸버그에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen nasceu em 15 de maio de 1180, em Mechelen, Antuérpia, Bélgica, seu pai, Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Sophia van Loon, tinha 17 anos. Ela casou-se com Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT aproximadamente 1216, em Tilburg, Brabant, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ela faleceu em 10 de abril de 1243, em Tilburg, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen родилась 15 мая 1180 г. в местности мечелен, антвирпен, Бельгия, её отцу по имени Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Sophia van Loon, 17. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT около 1216 г. в местности тилбург, брабант, Нидерланды. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Она умерла 10 апреля 1243 г. в местности тилбург, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Countess Agnes Waltherus Berthout van Mechelen Keerbergen nació el 15 de mayo de 1180, en Mechelen, Amberes, Bélgica, su papá, Graaf Wouter Berthout III Gerard, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Sophia van Loon, tenía 17 años. Se casó con Arnoldus Woutersz BAC TOT BAESCOT aproximadamente 1216, en Tilburg, Brabant, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 10 de abril de 1243, en Tilburg, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, a la edad de 62 años.
